---
title: "Machine Learning Model Evaluation and Resampling"
subtitle: "Module 3 — Cal Poly Pomona · Applied Machine Learning"
listing:
  contents:
    - we03_eval_resamp_slides.qmd
    - we03_eval_resamp_codebook.qmd
  type: grid
  sort: false
  grid-columns: 2
  fields: [title, description, reading-time]
  field-display-names:
    reading-time: "Estimated time"
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true
---

This module covers the two essential questions every machine learning
model must answer: *how good is it?* and *how do we know that estimate
is trustworthy?*

**Chapter 9** gives you the measurement instruments — regression metrics
(RMSE, R², MAE) and classification metrics (confusion matrix, sensitivity,
specificity, ROC/AUC) — using the `yardstick` package.

**Chapter 10** explains why a single measurement isn't enough, and
introduces resampling (cross-validation, bootstrap) via the `rsample`
package to get stable, honest performance estimates from the training
data alone.

Work through the materials in the order listed below.
